title: Model Tracking & MLflow Quiz
description: Test your knowledge on Model Tracking, MLflow components, and MLOps principles.

questions.question: In traditional software engineering, code is versioned. In Machine Learning, what must be tracked to guarantee reproducibility?

options.text: Code, Data, and Hyperparameters.
CPU architecture, RAM speed, and power consumption.
Only the Python file containing the `fit()` method.
API response times and bandwidth.

questions.question: Which MLflow component is responsible for saving training loss, accuracy metrics, and hyperparameters during an experiment run?

options.text: MLflow Projects
MLflow Tracking
MLflow Registry
MLflow Deployments

questions.question: What is the function of the MLflow Model Registry?

options.text: It stores user credentials for accessing cloud databases.
It acts as a centralized store to manage the lifecycle, versioning, and stage transitions (e.g., Staging to Production) of ML models.
It downloads big data sets from Kaggle directly into the notebook.
It automatically generates a React frontend for the model.

questions.question: What is the primary benefit of MLflow's `autolog()` feature?

options.text: It automatically cleans missing data in Pandas DataFrames.
It automatically adjusts learning rates dynamically during training.
It logs parameters, metrics, and models automatically for supported frameworks without requiring manual `.log_param()` calls.
It automatically deploys the model to AWS seamlessly.

questions.question: Why would a Data Engineer need to interact with an MLflow Model Registry?

options.text: To pull the specific model tagged as 'Production' to execute large-scale batch scoring jobs inside a data pipeline.
To alter the internal weights of the neural network using SQL.
To stream user clicks directly into the Registry.
To visualize the pipeline's DAG structure using Airflow.
